experimentGuidance.singleZ: Start with |+z⟩ (the default): the UP counter should get every atom. Switch to |+x⟩ or Random to see other statistics.
experimentGuidance.singleX: With |+z⟩ prepared, an X measurement is 50/50. Try |+x⟩ next — then UP should get every atom.
experimentGuidance.singleY: With |+z⟩ prepared, a Y measurement is also 50/50. Try preparing |+x⟩ and compare with an X analyzer.
experimentGuidance.singleN: Prepare |+z⟩, open Angles… on the analyzer, and vary θ. UP should follow Malus’s law: P(+n̂) = cos²(θ/2).
experimentGuidance.zThenX: With |+z⟩ prepared, the first SGz sends everything UP. That beam is still |+z⟩, so SGx on it is 50/50.
experimentGuidance.zThenY: With |+z⟩ prepared, the first SGz sends everything UP. That beam is still |+z⟩, so SGy on it is 50/50 — same story as Z then X on the third axis.
experimentGuidance.xThenZ: Prepare |+x⟩ so the first SGx sends everything UP; then SGz on that beam is 50/50. Compare with |+z⟩ prepared: order and preparation both matter.
experimentGuidance.zThenZ: With |+z⟩ prepared, both SGz analyzers send every atom UP — repeating the same basis never flips the state.
experimentGuidance.spinFilter: The first SGz has its DOWN exit blocked. With |+z⟩ nothing is lost; switch to |+x⟩ or Random and watch the Lost to filters readout climb.
experimentGuidance.threePolarizers: Prepare |+z⟩. Unlike the interferometer, each stage collapses: the middle SGx is 50/50, and the final SGz on the UP branch is again 50/50.
experimentGuidance.interferometer: Prepare |+z⟩. With Watch off, recombining both SGx paths restores |+z⟩ at the final SGz. Turn Watch on and interference vanishes.
experimentGuidance.blockedArm: Prepare |+z⟩. One SGx arm is blocked, so interference is gone even with Watch off — removing a path is not the same as recording which-path.
experimentGuidance.magnet: Prepare |+z⟩, then dial the magnet field to precess the spin before SGx. Counts should oscillate with field strength.
experimentGuidance.magnetIdentity: Field starts at 72 (one full turn): U(2π) = I, so SGx should match the no-magnet case. Try 36 (half turn) next.
experimentGuidance.recombination: Prepare |+z⟩. Merging selected paths before the next analyzer can restore coherence — compare Watch on versus off.
experimentGuidance.unknownLab: Mystery state Unknown #1 is prepared. Use the counters (try Z, then X or Y) to infer which eigenstate it is — without peeking at the Bloch sphere.
experimentGuidance.spin1Intro: Enable Spin 1 in Preferences (or ?spinOne). A Z analyzer then splits into three beams (+1 / 0 / −1). Pair with Recombination next.
experimentGuidance.custom: Prepare a named eigenstate near the source, then build your own apparatus: drag devices, wire ports, and try blocking an exit.

statePreparation.title: Prepared State
statePreparation.unknownHidden: Mystery state — fire atoms to infer it from the counters.
statePreparation.randomMixture: Random mixture: each basis state is equally likely.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows an initial-state panel near the particle source, wired through Stern–Gerlach analyzers and magnets to particle counters on the experiment board, with optional exit blockers. When enabled in Preferences, a quantum-system chooser appears on the source. Animated magenta dots represent atoms in flight. In Custom mode you can add devices, drag them into place, and wire output ports to input ports.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has an experiment preset chooser with apparatus notation and guidance, watch and expected-value toggles, analytic Do-N batch buttons, and Reset Counts. Reset All returns the simulation to its starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Choose an experiment, set the initial state near the source, optionally block an analyzer exit, then fire atoms through the analyzers and compare counter statistics with the expected values.

a11y.currentDetailsPattern: {{experiment}}, {{system}} system. Initial state: {{initialState}}. Atoms detected: {{total}}. Watch is {{watchStatus}}. Lost probability: {{deadEnd}}%.

controls.expectedValuesCheckbox: Show expected-value lines on the counters
controls.watchCheckbox: Watch which path each atom takes
controls.magnetFieldSpinnerHelp: Sets the precession dial from 0 to 99; a full spin rotation completes every 72 steps. This is a dial position, not a physical field strength in tesla or gauss.
controls.systemRadioGroup: Quantum system on the particle source
controls.analyzerToolboxItem: Add an analyzer to the board
controls.magnetToolboxItem: Add a magnet to the board
controls.counterToolboxItem: Add a counter to the board
controls.userStateBasisRadioGroup: Basis used to enter the custom amplitudes

builder.outputPortPattern: Output port {{index}}, press Enter or Space to cycle its connection

keyboardHelp.fireAtom: Fire one atom from the source
keyboardHelp.moveDevice: Move a device on the board
keyboardHelp.dragWire: Cycle an output port's connection through the possible targets
keyboardHelp.blockerHelp: Choose Open or Block on an analyzer to filter a spin component

dialogs.userStateIntro: Choose a basis, then set the complex amplitude of each basis ket. Values are normalized automatically and converted into the Z basis used by the analyzers.
dialogs.howToUseStep1: Start with an experiment preset, or switch System to Custom and drag analyzers, magnets, and counters onto the board to build your own.
dialogs.howToUseStep2: Set the initial state near the source: |+z⟩ is the default. Try |−z⟩, |+x⟩, or |−x⟩ next; Random and Unknown are for mixture and mystery exercises later.
dialogs.howToUseStep3: Fire a single atom to watch it travel through the apparatus, or switch to Continuous and turn on Watch to see many at once.
dialogs.howToUseStep4: Turn on Expected Values and use the Do-N buttons to run large batches, then check whether the counter statistics converge to the prediction.
dialogs.howToUseStep5: Once you have the basics, try blocking an analyzer exit or changing the direction angles to see how the outcome shifts.

preferences.title: Simulation
preferences.spinOneEnableDescription: Shows a Spin ½ / Spin 1 chooser on the particle source. Off by default (spin-½ only).
